summary: Synthesize a radial color gradient about a center with explicit sorted color stops; t = clamp(|P − center| / radius, 0, 1) interpolated per channel in the declared color space. A non-positive radius is rejected.

inputs.name: extent_from
inputs.doc: The resource whose pixel extent the produced gradient image matches.

params.name: center_px
params.doc: The gradient center [x, y] in pixel coordinates (t = 0).

params.name: radius_px
params.doc: The gradient radius in pixels (t = 1 at this distance); strictly positive.

params.name: layout
params.doc: The channel layout of the produced image; stop colors carry one component per channel.

params.name: color
params.doc: The color encoding of the produced image; interpolation happens in this space.

params.name: range
params.doc: The reference-light range policy; display-referred bounds color stops to [0, 1].

params.name: alpha
params.doc: The alpha representation of the produced image.

params.name: stops
params.doc: The sorted color stops [{ position: p in [0,1], color: [per-channel] }, …], spanning position 0 to 1.

perceptual.reason: paint.radial_gradient is a closed-form per-channel interpolation verified by stop-exactness, monotonic radial parameterization, and degenerate-radius rejection; there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
